intro: Moranopteris andina, also known as the Andean maidenhair fern, is a species of vascular plant in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential treatments for respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Moranopteris andina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
